reasoning: Slot A wins on every axis: its five mechanisms (system black-box, seed-packet husbandry, wordless false-color emotional scan, a credibility-staking prediction market that grades the adults, and a negative-space map of what the instrument missed) sit on genuinely different conceptual axes and each is rendered concrete with quotable sample lines. The Bet Slip and False-Color Scan are the boldest single ideas in the set. Slot C edges out B because its Confidence Map (scoring calibration, with "confidently wrong" as the worst territory) and Open Bench (the child assembling and surviving a self-chosen jury) are sharply original and the five map cleanly to five named broken assumptions. Slot B is well-crafted and articulate, but Friction Log and Constellation overlap conceptually (both environment/network framings) and several ideas tread familiar ground, lowering its diversity and boldness. Slot D ranks last: it is competent but the most conventional set, its ideas (negative-space, time-capsule, financial ledger, weather/mood) echo the stronger versions in A/C/B, and it breaks frame by exposing meta-process and a literal file path, which deflates the craft and the imaginative illusion.
